coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Long run day in peak phase: target 90–110 minutes with 800–1200m of vert, mixing Z2 climbing with 3–4 surges at 85–90% max HR (race-relevant pace on steep ground). Saturday is your anchor day—this is where you build the specific fitness that matters on Kranjska Gora's technical alpine terrain. Focus on foot placement and rhythm on descents; don't cruise downhills, but don't trash your quads either. Finish stronger than you start—your last 15 minutes should feel controlled but pushing, not cooked.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Eat a substantial breakfast 90–120 minutes before you start: 60–80g carbs (oats, banana, toast), 20–25g protein, low fiber. During the run, consume 45–60g carbs per hour via gels, chews, or sports drink—your sparse Alpine aid stations won't wait for you to bonk. Post-run within 30 minutes: 40g carbs + 20–25g protein to arrest the muscle damage window. Given your travel schedule, prep your carbs the night before so you're not scrambling Saturday morning.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: You're in week 18 of a 22-week block with zero recovery week buffer—sleep is non-negotiable. Target 7.5–8.5 hours minimum tonight; your CNS and glycogen repletion demand it more than any supplement will. Post-run, spend 8–10 minutes on lower-leg mobility (calves, shins, plantar fascia) since altitude + vert fatigue stiffens the dorsiflexors. If traveling for work, avoid the hotel gym and do nothing—active recovery Monday is your allocated day.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 111 days out and entering the zone where fitness ceiling matters less than durability. Today's run is a dress rehearsal for race-day effort distribution: treat the middle climb as a tactical moment where you assert control, not panic. The Julian Alps will demand sharp decision-making on fatigue; Saturday's surges teach your brain to separate true limits from discomfort. Finish today knowing you executed the plan. That's the only metric that counts right now.
